segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Right, so does anyone have any questions ...
segments.zh: 好了，那么大家有什么问题吗……

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Hi Chantal.
segments.zh: 嗨，尚塔尔。你觉得刚才的讲座怎么样？

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: What did you think of the talk, then?

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: Hi Hugo.
segments.zh: 嗨，雨果。我换了座位之后觉得还不错。

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: I thought it was good once I'd moved seats.

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Oh – were the people beside you chatting or something?
segments.zh: 哦——是不是你旁边的人在聊天什么的？

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: It wasn't that.
segments.zh: 不是的。我去得早，这样就能有个座位不用站着，但后来有个家伙正好坐在我前面，他个子特别高！

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: I went early so that I'd get a seat and not have to stand, but then this guy sat right in front of me and he was so tall!

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: It's hard to see through people's heads, isn't it?
segments.zh: 透过别人的脑袋看东西确实很难，对吧？

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: Impossible!
segments.zh: 根本不可能！不过，回答你的问题，我觉得真的很有趣，尤其是演讲者关于就业市场的那部分。

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: Anyway, to answer your question, I thought it was really interesting, especially what the speaker said about the job market.

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Me too.
segments.zh: 我也这么觉得。我是说，我们都知道我们即将进入一个竞争非常激烈的领域，所以很明显我们可能很难找到工作。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: I mean we know we're going into a really competitive field so it's obvious that we may struggle to get work.

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: That's right – and we know we can't all have that 'dream job'.
segments.zh: 没错——而且我们也知道不是每个人都能找到那份‘理想工作’。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Yeah, but it looks like there's a whole range of ... areas of work that we hadn't even thought of – like fashion journalism, for instance.
segments.zh: 是啊，但看起来有很多我们之前甚至没想过的工作领域——比如时尚新闻业。

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: Yeah – I wasn't expecting so many career options.
segments.zh: 是啊——我没想到会有这么多职业选择。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Mmm.
segments.zh: 嗯。总的来说，她的信息挺有冲击力的，是吧？

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Overall, she had quite a strong message, didn't she?

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: She did.
segments.zh: 确实。她一直说‘我知道你们都这么想，但是……’然后告诉我们真实情况是怎样的。

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: She kept saying things like 'I know you all think this, but ...

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: ' and then she'd tell us how it really is.

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Perhaps she thinks students are a bit narrow-minded about the industry.
segments.zh: 也许她觉得学生对行业看法有点狭隘。

words.def: 心胸狭隘的；目光短浅的

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: It was a bit harsh, though!
segments.zh: 不过有点苛刻！我们知道这个行业很艰难。

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: We know it's a tough industry.

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Yeah – and we're only first years, after all.
segments.zh: 是啊——而且我们毕竟才大一。还有很多要学的。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: We've got a lot to learn.

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: Exactly.
segments.zh: 没错。你觉得我们的中学教育是不是应该更侧重职业发展？

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: Do you think our secondary-school education should have been more career-focused?

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Well, we had numerous talks on careers, which was good, but none of them were very inspiring.
segments.zh: 嗯，我们听过很多职业讲座，这挺好的，但都不太鼓舞人心。他们本可以多请一些像今天这样的演讲者来跟我们聊聊。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: They could have asked more people like today's speaker to talk to us.

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: I agree.
segments.zh: 我同意。我们被告知了很多不同的职业——正好在我们需要的时候，但不是由真正懂行的专家来讲的。

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: We were told about lots of different careers – just when we needed to be, but not by the experts who really know stuff.

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: So did today's talk influence your thoughts on what career you'd like to take up in the future?
segments.zh: 那今天的演讲有没有影响你对未来职业的想法？

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: Well, I promised myself that I'd go through this course and keep an open mind till the end.
segments.zh: 嗯，我向自己保证，我会完成这门课程，并且一直保持开放的心态直到最后。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: But I think it's better to pick an area of the industry now and then aim to get better and better at it.
segments.zh: 但我认为最好是现在就选一个行业领域，然后努力变得越来越擅长。

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: Well, I think we'll just have to differ on that issue!
segments.zh: 嗯，我想我们只能在这个问题上保留不同意见了！

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: One thing's for certain, though.
segments.zh: 不过有一点是肯定的。根据她说的，我们会在行业里做很长时间的无薪助理。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: From what she said, we'll be unpaid assistants in the industry for quite a long time.

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: Mmm.
segments.zh: 嗯。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: I'm prepared for that, aren't you?
segments.zh: 我已经准备好了，你呢？

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: Actually, I'm not going to accept that view.
segments.zh: 实际上，我不打算接受那个观点。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Really?
segments.zh: 真的吗？但她知道情况就是这样——而且其他人都这么说。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: But she knows it's the case – and everyone else says the same.

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: That doesn't mean it has to be true for me.
segments.zh: 那并不意味着对我来说也一定是真的。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好吧。嗯——我希望你是对的！

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Well – I hope you're right!

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: I thought the speaker's account of her first job was fascinating.
segments.zh: 我觉得演讲者讲述她的第一份工作经历非常吸引人。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Yeah – she admitted she was lucky to get work being a personal dresser for a musician.
segments.zh: 是啊——她承认自己很幸运，能成为一位音乐家的私人服装师。她甚至没有申请那份工作，结果却得到了一份为她挑选所有衣服的差事。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: She didn't even apply for the job and there she was getting paid to choose all his clothes.

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: It must have felt amazing – though she said all she was looking for back then was experience, not financial reward.
segments.zh: 那感觉一定很棒——尽管她说她当时追求的只是经验，而不是金钱回报。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Mmm.
segments.zh: 嗯。然后他太刻薄了，竟然说她更关心自己的外表而不是他的！

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: And then he was so mean, telling her she was more interested in her own appearance than his!

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: But – she did realise he was right about that, which really made me think.
segments.zh: 但是——她确实意识到他在这点上是对的，这让我深思。我总在考虑自己的穿着，但现在我明白了，你应该把注意力放在客户身上！她显然后悔丢了那份工作。

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: I'm always considering my own clothes but now I can see you should be focusing on your client!

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: She obviously regretted losing the job.

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Well, as she said, she should have hidden her negative feelings about him, but she didn't.
segments.zh: 嗯，正如她所说，她本应该隐藏对他的负面情绪，但她没有。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: It was really brave the way she picked herself up and took that job in retail.
segments.zh: 她振作起来并接受了那份零售工作，真是勇敢。在那之后还愿意在商店工作！

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Fancy working in a shop after that!

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: Yeah – well, she recommended we all do it at some point.
segments.zh: 是啊——她建议我们每个人在某个时候都去做做。我想作为设计师，你会了解到一些有用的信息，比如普通购物者的体型大小。

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: I guess as a designer you'd get to find out some useful information, like how big or small the average shopper is.

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: I think that's an issue for manufacturers, not designers.
segments.zh: 我认为那是制造商的问题，而不是设计师的。不过，了解市场上是否有缺口会很有用——你知道，就是那种没有商家库存但消费者在寻找的商品。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: However, it would be useful to know if there's a gap in the market – you know, an item that no one's stocking but that consumers are looking for.

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: Yeah, people don't give up searching.
segments.zh: 是啊，人们不会放弃寻找。如果东西不合适，他们也会拿回店里退换。

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: They also take things back to the store if they aren't right.

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 是啊。想象一下，你在高档服装店工作，结果发现店里卖的衣服被退回来，因为……洗一次就散架了！

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Imagine you worked in an expensive shop and you found out the garments sold there were being returned because they ... fell apart in the wash!

segments.speaker: CHANTAL
segments.en: Yeah, it would be good to know that kind of thing.
segments.zh: 是啊，知道这种事情挺好的。

segments.speaker: HUGO
segments.en: Yeah.
segments.zh: 嗯。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: For my presentation today I want to tell you about how groups of elephants have been moved and settled in new reserves.
segments.zh: 今天我想向大家介绍的是，如何将象群迁移到新的保护区并安置下来。这被称为“易地保护”，近年来在非洲的马拉维得到了实施。这么做的原因是某些地区大象数量过多。数量过多是个好问题，而且我们通常很少听说。在马拉维的马杰特国家公园，大象曾因偷猎者猎取象牙而灭绝。但2003年，公园重新引进了大象，并实施了有效的执法。自那以后，没有一头大象被偷猎。在这个安全的环境中，大象数量激增。繁殖非常成功，以至于大象数量超过了公园的承载能力。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This is known as translocation and has been carried out in Malawi in Africa in recent years.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The reason this is being done is because of overpopulation of elephants in some areas.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Overpopulation is a good problem to have and not one we tend to hear about very often.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In Malawi's Majete National Park the elephant population had been wiped out by poachers, who killed the elephants for their ivory.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But in 2003, the park was restocked and effective law enforcement was introduced.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Since then, not a single elephant has been poached.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: In this safe environment, the elephant population boomed.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Breeding went so well that there were more elephants than the park could support.

items.prompt: What problem did Chantal have at the start of the talk?

pairs.note: 问题具体化为‘他太高了’，与题干‘problem’对应。

pairs.q: at the start of the talk
pairs.note: ‘开始演讲时’对应‘去得早’，表示时间点。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到‘去得早’和‘他太高了’，分别对应题干中的‘at the start of the talk’和‘problem’，从而锁定答案A。

items.prompt: What were Hugo and Chantal surprised to hear about the job market?

pairs.note: ‘没想到’就是‘惊讶地听说’的另一种说法
‘工作领域’和‘就业市场’意思相近

paraphrase.explain: 听到‘hadn't even thought of’对应题干‘surprised to hear’，‘areas of work’对应‘job market’，从而定位到答案句。

items.prompt: Hugo and Chantal agree that the speaker's message was

pairs.note: agree 对应 We know，表示双方认同

paraphrase.explain: 定位句是两人对话，'We know' 表明双方同意，'It' 指代 speaker's message，所以答案在描述 message 的形容词上。

items.prompt: What do Hugo and Chantal criticise about their school careers advice?

pairs.p: none of them were very inspiring
pairs.note: 批评即指出不足，原文说讲座不鼓舞人心，就是批评
学校职业建议对应职业讲座

paraphrase.explain: 题干问批评什么，定位句说讲座不鼓舞人心，且希望请更多像今天这样的演讲者，对应选项C（缺乏有趣的演讲者）。

items.prompt: When discussing their future, Hugo and Chantal disagree on

pairs.note: 题干说'不同意'，原文说'向自己保证'，暗示个人决定，没有直接分歧，但通过上下文体现不同观点。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句提到Hugo向自己保证保持开放心态，暗示他与Chantal可能意见不同，但未直接说明分歧点，需结合上下文判断。

items.prompt: How does Hugo feel about being an unpaid assistant?

pairs.note: 题干问感受，原文用'准备好'表达态度，是同意替换

paraphrase.explain: 通过题干关键词'unpaid assistant'定位到原文，再根据'How does Hugo feel'找到对应态度表达'I'm prepared for that'，从而选出答案A。

items.prompt: Which TWO mistakes did the speaker admit she made in her first job?

pairs.p: should have hidden her negative feelings
pairs.note: 题干问'错误'，原文用'本应隐藏负面情绪但没做到'来表述错误，是间接表达。
题干'admit'（承认）对应原文'she said'（她说），表示承认。

paraphrase.explain: 定位句开头'Well, as she said'直接对应题干'admit she made'，后面'hidden her negative feelings'是具体错误内容。

items.prompt: Which TWO mistakes did the speaker admit she made in her first job?
Which TWO pieces of retail information do Hugo and Chantal agree would be useful?
Which TWO pieces of retail information do Hugo and Chantal agree would be useful?
